description_long: This object is a disassembled wooden chaise, composed of multiple elongated planks and beams with visible joinery slots and tenons, suggesting a modular construction. The wood appears to be light-colored, possibly beech or maple, with a smooth, matte finish. The structure includes long side rails, shorter crossbars, and several support slats, all designed to interlock for stability. There are no visible cushions, upholstery, or decorative elements, emphasizing a minimalist and functional design. The legs are straight and rectangular, matching the rest of the frame, and there are no armrests or backrest segments present. The overall form is linear and geometric, suitable for outdoor or urban park settings.
description: A minimalist, disassembled wooden chaise with straight, rectangular beams and a smooth, light wood finish. The design is functional and lacks upholstery, armrests, or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing all wooden components laid out in an organized, exploded arrangement, clearly displaying the joinery slots and tenons.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth and thickness of the beams, with the joinery details visible on the ends of the planks.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the alignment of the support slats and the overall rectangular frame structure.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the length of the side rails and the modular nature of the assembly.
attribute_backrest_design: No backrest is present; the design is entirely flat and linear with no raised or segmented back support.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side.
attribute_leg_style: The legs are straight, rectangular wooden beams matching the rest of the frame, with no decorative elements or tapering.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: There is no visible upholstery or fabric; the surface is solid wood with a smooth, matte finish.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: No cushions are present; the seating surface is composed entirely of wooden slats and beams.
